data.title: Bloom Energy sinks as Jefferies cuts, with valuation reflecting high expectations amid uncertainty
data.description:
Bloom Energy (NYSE:BE) -11.7% in Wednesday's trading after Jefferies downgraded shares to Underperform from Hold with a $31 price target, citing limited visibility into post-2026 growth
and "some early signs of over-exuberance."

Bloom Energy (BE) is indeed a leader in a promising niche, with many bullish assumptions facing practical constraints, Jefferies analysts led by Dushyant Ailani said, but risks to the downside
outweigh further upside at the stock's current valuation  at ~31x estimated FY 2027 EBITDA, pricing in aggressive growth.

"It is tough to justify a 30x multiple with no visibility on what 2027 and beyond looks like," the analysts wrote, and applying a more reasonable multiple implies volume levels that "seem quite
difficult to achieve," with the biggest debate involving how quickly the company can sign deals - "as management has noted previously, it takes time to ink bigger deals. While we don't underestimate BE's ability to get to 1 GW-plus eventually, we don't see that playing out near term."

Besides a press release with substantive data points on deals, Jefferies sees the next catalyst for the stock coming in February 26 when Bloom Energy (NYSE:BE) discloses its backlog, but "until then, we question the support level of the stock."

data.title: U.S. stocks falls as tech sector struggles, rate-cut uncertainties rattle markets
data.description:
Wall Street's major indexes were lower on Wednesday, as U.S. technology stocks weighed on the markets and uncertainty persisted among investors.

The S&P 500 (SP500) was last -0.4% in afternoon trade, while the Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) was also -0.5%, and the Dow (DJI) was -0.3%.

“Investors trimmed their exposure following a comment from Fed Chair Jerome Powell [saying] ‘equity prices are fairly highly valued.’ He went on to say that the current situation wasn’t a time ‘… of elevated financial stability risks,’ but investors decided that discretion is the better part of valour and took the opportunity to lighten up a touch,” said David Morrison, senior market analyst at Trade Nation.

On the tech front, OpenAI is considering renting AI chips from Nvidia (NVDA) -1% to reduce costs associated with the new $100B data center partnership between the two companies.

“This investment was welcomed in many quarters as evidence of the faith that the most valuable company in history has in the future of generative AI. But others have questioned the circular nature of the investment, particularly as the journey to monetizing the ongoing funding in AI is far from clear,” said Morrison.

“On top of this, the assumption is that Nvidia (NVDA), and other leading chip companies, have an unbridgeable moat around them. But as was demonstrated with DeepSeek at the beginning of this year, it’s quite possible that Chinese companies come up with cheaper alternatives to Nvidia’s products, particularly since U.S. bans on selling specific chips to China are driving Chinese innovation in this area,” he added.

Micron Technology (MU) edged lower -3.2%, even after its outlook eclipsed estimates on AI data center demand. Adobe (ADBE) -3% and Oracle (ORCL) -3% are also pushing the S&P 500 (SP500) and the Nasdaq (COMP:IND) lower.

Over in the bond market, the 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) rose 4 basis points to 4.15%, while the 2-year yield (US2Y) rose 2 basis points to 3.61%. A $70B, 5-Year Note Auction slightly tailed.

On the economy, new home sales surged 20.5% MoM in August to 800k. Consensus was for 649k.

Business uncertainty about revenue growth rose to 3.83% in September, up from 3.77% in August, according to an Atlanta Fed survey. Also, CFOs recorded greater optimism in Q3, the Richmond Fed survey showed.

U.S. stocks ended in the red on Tuesday, breaking from their three-day all-time high closes, after Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell largely reiterated his message from last week that policymakers will have a difficult path ahead on interest rates.

data.title: Keurig Dr Pepper's plan to split business presents execution complexities -- Barclays
data.description:
Keurig Dr Pepper’s (NASDAQ:KDP) decision to split its beverage and coffee business will prove to be the right move, Barclays says, but the complex mechanics of the separation presents “elevated noise and uncertainty” over the next 12 months, leading analyst Lauren Lieberman to downgrade the stock to Equal Weight from Underweight and lower her price target by 33% to $26 to represent the sum-of-the-parts valuation.

Keurig Dr Pepper (NASDAQ:KDP) said it plans to separate its coffee and beverage units into two entities once its acquisition of JDE Peet's is complete. The coffee business will contain brands like Keurig and Green Mountain, and the newly acquired Peet’s Coffee, with ~$16B in annual net sales.

The beverage business will house brands including Dr Pepper, Canada Dry, and 7UP with over ~$11B in annual net sales.

“There has been minimal pushback from investors on the notion of this proposed division of assets making more sense than the current setup – the problem is really how we get there,” Lieberman says in her note to clients.

While there are lingering questions regarding the separation, Barclays’ Equal Weight rating reflects the balanced momentum in KDP’s beverage and coffee businesses with the uncertainty to come from the transaction.

“KDP’s fundamental setup no longer offers the compelling relative clarity that we had liked in the name previously,” Lieberman says, and that there will be some form of structural change to the beverage business post-separation given the different route-to-market and manufacturing models under one roof.

As for the coffee business, Lieberman likes the greater scale and diversification, but the combined business “still has its work cut out for it,” especially considering JDE Peet’s “checkered performance” since its 2020 IPO.

Lieberman’s revised price target (from $39) reflects 2% downside from KDP’s current share price, as “we see the uncertainty and complexity tied to the announced transactions as largely baked into the stock at this point,” she adds.

Since announcing the JDE Peet’s acquisition at the end of August, Keurig Dr Pepper (NASDAQ:KDP) shares have lost as much as 17% in value and continue to circle the drain at the 5 ½ year low.

data.title: Companies are following the ‘Invest in AI now, ask the ROI question later’ – Susquehanna’s Mehdi Hosseini
data.description:
Mehdi Hosseini, senior equity research analyst at Susquehanna, believes companies are following a “spend now, ask ROI later” approach in artificial intelligence investments, suggesting this trend could continue longer than previously anticipated.

In an interview with CNBC, Hosseini noted that questions about return on investment for massive AI spending are being pushed out to “sometime in '27” rather than 2026 as he had initially predicted.

Unlike previous cycles that were driven by distributors and OEMs (original equipment manufacturer), the current AI boom features major tech companies like NVIDIA (NVDA), AMD (AMD), and Broadcom (AVGO) as the primary buyers.

“This is why this cycle is sustainable throughout [2025-2026], and it may even sustain into 2027,” Hosseini said, highlighting the unusual durability of the current market conditions.

The concentration of buying power among major AI companies is creating unique market dynamics throughout the supply chain. When questioned about whether this customer concentration makes the cycle more vulnerable, Hosseini countered that if NVIDIA (NVDA) isn’t being penalized for its own customer concentration, then “Micron (MU) shouldn’t be penalized for that because that’s the downside risk to the overall AI spending.”

For memory manufacturer Micron (MU) specifically, Hosseini sees significant upside potential. “What investors haven’t realized is not only supply is going to remain tight, the next generation of AI chip – Rubin (a new class of GPU purpose-built for massive-context processing) – is going to require even more advanced DRAM (dynamic random-access memory), and Sanjay [Mehrotra, Micron’s CEO] is going to be able to extend margins towards 55% or high 50s,” he said, projecting Micron Technology (MU) could reach $200 per share.

Despite his optimism, Hosseini acknowledged potential risks with the vendor financing model emerging in the AI space.

“Vendor financing is very concerning… I think at some point, this is going to backfire,” he cautioned, drawing parallels to similar patterns he observed in the solar industry in 2008-2009.

However, he believes any significant downturn has been delayed: “Spend is spent, and then you ask the ROI question later.”

data.title: BlackBerry Q2 earnings on deck: What to expect
data.description:
Blackberry (NYSE:BB) is scheduled to announce Q2 earnings results on Thursday, before markets open. 

Wall Street expects the company to post EPS of $0.01 on revenue of $122.03 million, implying a fall of nearly 16% during the quarter.

After the Waterloo-based company's exit from the smartphone market, it has transitioned into selling software and cybersecurity services for devices and autonomous vehicles. Earlier in June, the company boosted its annual forecast in the range of $508M to $538M, compared to the consensus estimate of $513.05M.

Seeking Alpha analyst Chris Lau pointed out that BlackBerry’s growth is supported by its QNX software and secure communications, with strong opportunities in the automotive and government sectors despite macro and competitive risks.

“A guidance hike might help the stock rise further from here. Conversely, the stock could drop if the guidance does not increase,” Lau added.

However, another Seeking Alpha analysis by Joseph Parrish noted that the QNX segment could benefit from self-driving trends, yet automotive cyclicality and tariff risks limit near-term upside.

RBC expects BlackBerry to report Q2 results slightly above consensus, citing a more stable macro environment, and expects the company to reiterate its FY26 revenue guidance and raise its adjusted EBITDA outlook.

Over the past three months, EPS estimates have seen one upward revision compared to one downward revision, while revenue estimates have seen two upward revisions versus two downward moves, reflecting concerns about a cautious market and macroeconomic headwinds.

While current estimates are subdued, the company has delivered positive surprises: Over the last 2 years, BlackBerry has beaten EPS estimates 88% of the time and has beaten revenue estimates 75% of the time.

The stock has risen nearly 13% so far this year, almost in line with the broader S&P 500 Index.

data.title: Riot stock climbs after Roth boosts price target to Street high
data.description:
Riot Platforms (NASDAQ:RIOT) stock jumped 6.4% in Wednesday afternoon earnings after Roth analyst Darren Aftahi raised his price estimate on the crypto and energy stock to a Street high, based on increasing demand for power and as the company nears active marketing of its Corsicana site.

Riot (NASDAQ:RIOT) is a vertically integrated bitcoin (BTC-USD) mining company that engages in enhancing its power infrastructure to support efficient and large-scale bitcoin-mining operations.

The company's stock surge came as the price of bitcoin (BTC-USD) rose 1.4% over the past 24 hours to ~$113.8K.

With the site master design expected by the end of Q3, the company may take its passive marketing "to a more active level" with potential clients, the analyst said.

"While management was tight-lipped about timelines, we believe they are getting closer to more actively marketing the site and its potential tangible designs, with further development a key part in negotiations," Aftahi wrote in a note to clients.

Roth, which has a Buy rating on Riot (NASDAQ:RIOT), increased its 12-month price target on the stock to $42.00 from $17.50. Before the PT increase, the stock's average price target was $17.37.

The firm's Buy rating on Riot (RIOT) compares with the SA Quant rating of Strong Buy, the average Wall Street rating of Strong Buy, and the average SA Analyst rating of Buy.

data.title: FB Financial appoints CFO Michael Mettee to additional post of COO
data.description:
FB Financial (NYSE:FBK) announced on Wednesday the appointment of CFO Michael Mettee to the additional post of COO, effective October 1.

The financial holding company said COO Scott Tansil is set to be named chief business and operations officer, also effective the same day.

data.title: Mirum draws new Buy at TD Cowen on growth prospects
data.description:
TD Cowen initiated its coverage of Mirum Pharmaceuticals (NASDAQ:MIRM) with a Buy recommendation on Wednesday, noting undervaluation in the stock, given the rare disease drug developer's growth prospects.

Analyst Joseph Thome projected $1.08B in peak sales for Mirum's (NASDAQ:MIRM) lead asset, Livmarli, approved in the U.S. to treat itching associated with pediatric cholestatic liver diseases, Alagille syndrome, and progressive familial intrahepatic cholestasis.

Given that a Phase 3 trial for the oral therapy is underway targeting pruritus in other rare cholestatic liver diseases, "we estimate that all three opportunities could generate peak sales of ~$1.08B,” Thome wrote, citing KOL input.

Additionally, the analyst projected $258M in peak sales for the company’s bile acid portfolio comprising its ultra-rare disease therapies, Ctexli and Cholbam, indicated for cerebrotendinous xanthomatosis and bile acid synthesis disorder, respectively.

Thome also modeled $591M in peak sales for Mirum’s (NASDAQ:MIRM) experimental drug volixibat, targeted at pruritus in adult cholestatic liver diseases. “We view shares as undervalued for Livmarli, the bile acid portfolio, and volixibat opportunities,” the analyst wrote with a $95 per share target on the stock.

data.title: Wells Fargo’s Darrell Cronk highlights four signals above market noise for long-term success
data.description:
Investors should focus on four key signals that point to extended market growth through 2026 and beyond, according to Darrell L. Cronk, president at Wells Fargo Investment Institute.

Cronk, who is also the CIO of the bank’s Wealth & Investment Management group, said, “When I force myself to rise above the noise of day-to-day markets, I see bright secular trends on the horizon that have the potential to extend this bull market.”

Fiscal clarity stands as the first signal above trade noise, with legislation like the One Big Beautiful Bill Act signed by U.S. President Donald Trump providing “sizable increases in the expensing of equipment, higher deduction limits for research and development and manufacturing infrastructure,” Cronk said.

He emphasized that this fiscal clarity enables “companies and individuals to quantify the tax and financial impacts,” which has boosted markets, while the Infrastructure Investment and Jobs Act from the Biden administration has only allocated about 50% of its $1.2 trillion so far.

The AI revolution represents another powerful signal, with McKinsey & Company estimating “AI infrastructure spend alone are close to $7 trillion by 2030, driven by explosive demand for data centers, compute power, and semiconductors,” Cronk said.

“The challenge of this AI revolutionary cycle is that it is larger, moving faster, and more compressed than past periods of seismic economic and technological change,” he said, adding that fully one-third of all S&P 500 (SP500) capital expenditures this year will come from the technology sector (XLK).

Monetary policy clarity provides the third significant signal, with Cronk pointing out that “historically, when the Fed cuts rates without an impending recession, equities have done very well.”

He noted that current conditions lack typical recession warning signs. “The Financials sector (XLF) of the S&P 500, as of September 22, is at an all-time high, and there are only a very few times when the Fed has cut interest rates with Financials at or close to their all-time highs.”

Market breadth expansion represents the fourth key signal, as Cronk observed that “while a few mega stocks can create a lot of noise, most large-cap stocks today are reasonably priced.”

He pointed to approximately $7 trillion in money market balances sitting at all-time highs, noting that “if the Fed lowers rates, as we expect, those funds are likely to look for a new home,” while merger-and-acquisition activity has increased over 30% year-over-year and IPOs (IPO) are up over 60%.

Cronk concluded that distinguishing between market noise and valuable information is what separates wise investors from the herd.

“For investors, the signal, sharpened by tracking fundamental trends over time, is the real focus. The noise, delivered daily in headlines and sound bites, simply confuses,” he said, emphasizing that rarely do so many favorable conditions align simultaneously to create such a positive environment for markets.

data.title: EV sales are rising before the tax credit expires—but what happens next?
data.description:
The U.S. automobile industry disruption will see major disruption next week when the federal EV tax credit expires on September 30. The federal EV tax credit provides up to $7,500 for qualifying new electric vehicles and up to $4,000 for qualifying used EVs. Buyers must meet income requirements and also select vehicles that satisfy price caps, final assembly, and battery sourcing requirements.

Ahead of the expiration, U.S. EV sales jumped 18% and hit a record high in August, according to Cox Automotive. Used EV sales soared 59% for the same period. EV demand is reported to be strong through September, matching August’s elevated activity as buyers hurry to qualify for the EV credit. Hyundai (HYMTF), Toyota Motor (TM), and Honda (HMC) have all pointed to the impact of the expiring tax credit in their recent sales updates.

New data from Cars.com indicated that nearly half of consumers considering buying an EV are now accelerating their purchases. Cars.com said demand on its site is up 33% for new EVs compared with this time a year ago. "Shoppers are moving quickly to lock in savings, and Cars.com’s Top EV Picks, including the Hyundai Ioniq 6 and Ioniq 5, Kia EV9, and Chevrolet Equinox EV, show there are strong options for every type of buyer," highlighted the company.

Notably, high-demand EV models are selling out quickly, which has left some dealers working with limited stock on some models. Auto dealers are trying to thread the needle of not oversupplying their lots on the expectation that demand will fall on October 1.

The top ten sellers of electric vehicles are Tesla (NASDAQ:TSLA), General Motors (GM), Hyundai (HYMTF), Ford Motor (F), BMW (BMWYY), Volkswagen (OTCPK:VWAGY), Nissan (OTCPK:NSANY), Rivian Automotive (NASDAQ:RIVN), and Toyota (TM). Further down the list is EV pure play Lucid Group (LCID).

 

Related tickers: Carvana (CVNA), CarMax (KMX), Lithia Motors (LAD), AutoNation (AN), Asbury Automotive Group (ABG), CarGurus (CARG), Group 1 Automotive (GPI), Sonic Automotive (SAH), Penske Automotive Group (PAG), Vroom (VRM), Rush Enterprises (RUSHA), Shift Technologies (OTC:SFTGQ), Driven Brands (DRVN), ACV Auctions (ACVA), Cars.com (CARS), Tesla (NASDAQ:TSLA), Toyota Motor (TM), Honda Motor (HMC), Nissan (OTCPK:NSANY), Openlane (KAR), Hyundai (HYMTF), General Motors (GM), Rivian Automotive (NASDAQ:RIVN), Lucid Group (LCID), Stellantis (STLA), and Ford Motor (F).
